input.background: The odds ratio for the case-control data in Table 18.15 is $\widehat{O R}=\frac{96 \cdot 666}{104 \cdot 109}=5.64$.
input.question: Interpret the odds ratio in the context of esophageal cancer risk between high-alcohol and low-alcohol consumers.

output.method: 分类或发生概率影响因素解读
output.answer: This suggests that high-alcohol consumers have 5.6 times the rate of esophageal cancer compared to low-alcohol consumers.

Alcohol_consumption.description: The level of alcohol consumption categorized as high (≥ 80 grams/day) and low (< 80 grams/day).

Esophageal_cancer_cases.description: The number of esophageal cancer cases among high and low alcohol consumers.

Esophageal_cancer_noncases.description: The number of non-cases of esophageal cancer among high and low alcohol consumers.

Odds_Ratio.description: The calculated odds ratio indicating the odds of esophageal cancer risk between high-alcohol and low-alcohol consumers.
